name: Lamb-And-Sausage Couscous
recipeIngredient: 3 lb Lamb shoulder - cut into 2-in cubes
4 medium Carrots - peeled and cut into 1/2-in rounds
6 cups Low-sodium chicken broth or Water
2 medium Zucchini - cut into rounds 1-in thick
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat oven to 325°F.
2. Pat the lamb dry and sprinkle with salt and pepper as desired.
3. Heat olive oil in a large covered casserole or Dutch oven over high heat on top of the stove.
4. Add the lamb, without crowding, in batches if necessary, and brown well on all sides. Remove pieces to a plate as they are done and reserve.
5. Repeat until all lamb is browned. Do not pour off fat.
6. Replace the casserole over medium heat.
7. Add onion, carrots, celery, and tomato paste. Cook, stirring occasionally, for 5 minutes.
8. Use your spoon to loosen and dissolve the brown bits stuck to the bottom of the casserole.
9. Add flour and cook, stirring, an additional minute.
10. Add wine, meat (and any juices on the plate), stock, cayenne, and cumin. Cover, bring to a boil, and place in the oven for 1 ¼ hours, or until lamb is tender.
11. Meanwhile, combine sausage and zucchini in an ovenproof dish, cover, and place in the oven until sausages are cooked, about 20 minutes.
12. Follow directions on the back of the couscous box to cook.
13. Remove stewed lamb and the sausage/vegetable mixture from the oven.
14. Using a slotted spoon, remove meat from the sauce and place it in a serving bowl.
15. Pour off and discard any excess fat from the sausages. Add sausages and vegetables to the lamb.
16. Pour the thin gravy through a fine strainer into the serving bowl and discard the vegetables and spices that remain in the strainer.
17. To serve, place couscous in another serving dish. Pour broth into a pitcher or soup tureen. Encourage guests to place couscous in their soup bowl, top with meat and vegetables, and moisten with soup.
